markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 174 **Price** 27 gp **Ammunition** [sling bullet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=72) **Activate**Interact --- Threads of iridescent golden silk wrap around the core of this magical [sling stone](/Weapons.aspx?ID=82). When you activate and shoot a _golden chrysalis_, rather than making an attack roll for your Strike, you cause the chrysalis to unwind in midair to reveal a magical butterfly that flies in a 30-foot line, scattering golden scale dust that hangs in the air for 1 minute. A creature that enters or begins its turn in the dust must attempt a DC 19 Reflex save or have its invisibility negated, becoming [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) instead of [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26). This effect applies both if the creature was already invisible and if it benefits from any new invisibility effects before the end of the invisibility negation effect from the _golden chrysalis_. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target's invisibility is negated for 2 rounds.
**Failure** The target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 minute and its invisibility is negated for 1 minute.
name: Golden Chrysalis

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Lion Claw](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1024)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 174

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

**Requirement** You're [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) by a creature and are a master in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15).
</row>

</column>

---

This dried claw from a mighty beast bestows upon you the ability of a predator. When you activate the claw, you learn to pounce on your prey in one fluid motion. You Stride and then Strike with the affixed weapon against one creature you were undetected by. You remain undetected by the creature until after you Strike.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Lion Claw
requirement_markdown: You're [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) by a creature and are a master in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Orchestral Brooch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1025)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 174

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You attempt a [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) check, but you haven't rolled yet

**Requirement** You're a master in Performance.
</row>

</column>

---

This silver brooch reverberates lightly with the sound of music every time anyone touches it. You can transform it into the shape of any chosen instrument when you Affix it. When you activate this talisman, your performance is accompanied by a grand procession of music that complements your own work, subject to your direction and intent. You receive a +1 status bonus to your Performance check. If you roll a success, you get a critical success instead.

 After being used, the talisman remains a mundane silver brooch with the appearance of the chosen instrument, though it possesses no remaining power.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Orchestral Brooch

    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Phoenix Flask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1026)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 174

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Once you ingest this strongly spiced, glowing red potion, blazing wings of a [phoenix](/Monsters.aspx?ID=330) sprout from your back and carry you through the air. For 1 minute, you gain a [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) speed of 40 feet. The first time each round that you Fly (including to hover in place), you shed burning feathers that deal 3d4 fire damage to all creatures in a 10-foot emanation at the end of your movement (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Phoenix Flask

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Potion of Grounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1027)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Electricity" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=56" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 174

**Price** 185 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Sparks flash within this amber syrup. Drinking this potion turns you into a living lightning rod for 1 minute, drawing nearby electricity to strike you instead of allies. You gain the following reaction while the effect lasts.

 **Divert Lightning** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56)) **Requirements** You aren't immune to electricity; **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet of you is targeted by an electricity effect or is in the area of an electricity effect. **Effect** You draw the electricity to yourself like a lightning rod in order to shield the target. If the triggering effect was targeted, it targets you instead of the triggering creature. If it was an area effect, it leaps from the triggering creature's space to fill your space instead. This doesn't divert lightning from any other creatures targeted by or in the area of the lightning. You're automatically hit by or automatically fail your save against the effect. You then can't Divert Lightning for 1d4 rounds.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Potion of Grounding

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Potion of Minute Echoes](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1028)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 175

**Price** 125 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

No matter how carefully you open this bottle, it always lets out an audible, echoing pop. For 1 minute after drinking this potion, you gain a +2 status bonus to Perception checks to hear. In addition, each time you [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84), your hearing becomes a [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) sense until the beginning of your next turn, allowing you to pinpoint creatures' locations and otherwise perceive the world in detail by listening to the sound of echoes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Potion of Minute Echoes

    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Potion of Stable Form](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1029)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 175

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This aromatic potion is brewed from the white flowers and black roots of the magical herb moly. When you drink this potion, it immediately attempts to counteract all hostile [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157) effects affecting you. For the next hour, you gain an item bonus against transmutation effects, which is greater against [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127) effects. If you roll a success against a polymorph effect during that time, you get a critical success instead.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Price** 180 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) level is 5, and the counteract check modifier is +17. The item bonus is +2, or +3 vs. polymorph.

level="2"

</column>

---

Two swirling liquids fill this flask, each slightly distinct in color and brightness from the other. When you drink this potion, you consume only half of the contents. If another willing creature consumes the remainder of the contents within 1 minute, your vitalities become linked for 1 minute from the moment the second one of you drinks. The two of you share breath, so as long as you're within 60 feet of one another, neither of you can begin [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) unless you're both suffocating. You both gain the following reaction.

 **Share Life** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** Your linked ally takes damage and is within 60 feet; **Effect** The ally takes half damage from the triggering effect (rounded down), and you lose a number of Hit Points equal to the remainder of the damage. You can't trigger this reaction to share damage caused by your ally using this reaction.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Potion of Shared Life

    level="1"

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Retrieval Prism](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1032)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 176

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) A basic retrieval prism must be attuned to a PC to function._

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>command</row></row>

 

 

**Requirement** You have a free hand.
</row>

</column>

---

This triangular prism showing swirling black patterns inside constantly moves around on your armor, no matter where you affix it. As part of the process of Affixing this Talisman, you attune it to a single item of 1 Bulk or less. When you activate this talisman, the attuned item immediately teleports into your hand.

 The _retrieval prism_ can't retrieve an item that's not on your plane, including one that's in an [extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=67) space like a [_bag of holding_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=249). If you haven't expended the talisman, you can attune it to a different item by Affixing the Talisman again.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"

</title>

</traits>

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Retrieval Prism (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 176

**Price** 200 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You don't need to attune the prism. It can retrieve any item in your possession of 1 Bulk or less, chosen when you activate the prism.
name: Retrieval Prism

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Shortbread Spy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1033)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Scrying" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=141" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 176

**Price** 40 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row>1 minute (Interact)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Though this item looks like a simple cookie in the shape of a humanoid, it springs to life once decorated with icing or other edible substances. The cookie then scrambles away at a Speed of 15 feet, returning to the same spot about 1 hour later, which gives it enough time to travel roughly a half-mile away and then return along the same path. The cookie spy is oblivious to your instructions and can't be given directions, instead following a path of its own choosing. Upon its return, it falls to the ground, never to move again.

 As long as you decorated the _shortbread spy_ with eyes, it gains normal vision, which it uses to see and magically record the sights along its path. Any creature that eats the cookie once it returns can then see what the spy saw. The images are relatively clear, but the passage of time is a bit muddied, so it might be difficult to tell when the cookie witnessed a given sight.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Shortbread Spy

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Topology Protoplasm](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1035)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Oil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=121" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 176

**Price** 70 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This slimy gel wriggles to the touch, as if covered by a multitude of imperceptible cilia. If applied to a creature or object no larger than 7 feet in any dimension, the protoplasm shifts it into an [ooze](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=79)-like state for 1 minute, allowing the subject to squash and stretch harmlessly. In this state, a creature that attempts a check to [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=31) uses the outcome one degree of success better than it rolls and can move its full Speed while Squeezing, and an object can fit through a space 2 feet across. One vial can cover a creature or object of up to Large size, but as each vial is made from a specific ooze, multiple vials can't be combined together to cover a larger object, as the two gels simply negate each other.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Topology Protoplasm

    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Standard"

[Vapor Sphere](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1036)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 176

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Reaction" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You trigger a trap's reaction or an enemy that was [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) by you makes an attack against you

**Requirement** You're an expert in the affixed armor and an expert in Reflex saves.
</row>

</column>

---

Within this strange glass sphere swirls a cloud of smoke that occasionally appears as if it's trying to escape. You react to a surprise encounter with haste, activating this talisman and causing your body to momentarily become like vapor. Against the trap's reaction or the attack, you gain resistance 25 to physical damage and are immune to precision damage. This effect also prevents the reaction or attack from physically moving you (such as falling down a pit or being knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31)), and after the reaction or attack, you can [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) 5 feet. When you end this flight, you leave your vaporous state and are exposed to any danger still at your location.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Vapor Sphere
requirement_markdown: You're an expert in the affixed armor and an expert in Reflex saves.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This waterskin coruscates with holy energy, causing it to slowly fill itself with special blessed water unique to the item. After using any of the activations, the waterskin is empty, but slowly refills itself. It becomes full enough to use again at the next dawn.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;    **Requirements** The _anointed waterskin_ is full; **Effect** You throw the _anointed waterskin_ up to 60 feet. The water explodes out of it, with the effects of [_holy cascade_](/Spells.aspx?ID=151) with a DC of 25.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;    **Requirements** The _anointed waterskin_ is full; **Effect** You drink the water within the waterskin, granting you the effects of [_bless_](/Spells.aspx?ID=25). As normal with the spell, you can increase the radius with a single action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (command, Interact);    **Requirements** The _anointed waterskin_ is full; **Effect** You decant the water, creating up to 10 vials of [_holy water_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=245). You must provide the vials.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Anointed Waterskin

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Archivist's Gaze](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1038)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 179

**Price** 24,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This strange contraption slides over your eyes, supernaturally sharpening your mind. While peering through it, you can feel some entity whispering to you, telling all sorts of things about the subject of your gaze. You gain a +3 item bonus to [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) (though some entities might grant a bonus to a different skill, as determined by your GM). In addition, when you employ an exploration tactic other than Investigating, you also gain the benefits of [Investigating](/Actions.aspx?ID=516) unless you choose not to.

 When you invest the spectacles, you either increase your Intelligence score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you the higher score. This gives you additional trained skills and languages, as normal for increasing your Intelligence score. You must select skills and languages the first time you invest the item, and whenever you invest the same _archivist's gaze_, you get the same skills and languages you chose the first time.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** Pushing the glasses up your nose and asking the entity for help, you cause the spectacles to cast either [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) or a 3rd-level [_comprehend language_](/Spells.aspx?ID=46) on you. Because the entity tied to the spectacles chooses which one, the GM picks whichever spell is most immediately useful in your current situation (and chooses the most useful language each time the item casts _comprehend language_). The spell lasts for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Archivist's Gaze

[Auspicious Scepter](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1039)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 179

</row>

</row>

---

This imperious _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [mace](/Weapons.aspx?ID=7)_ has a glowing orb in the shape of an eye set in its flanged head. When you succeed at a check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) about a creature after you've dealt it damage with the _auspicious scepter_, you learn one of its resistances in addition to any other information.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> command ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) **Frequency** once every 10 minutes; **Trigger** You Cast a divination spell;  **Effect** The next attack by a creature who was targeted by the triggering divination spell bypasses an amount of resistance equal to the spell's level. If the triggering spell targeted an enemy, this benefit instead applies to the next attack against that enemy, regardless of who makes it.

 This benefit is lost if it isn't used by the end of your next turn. If more than one type of resistance would apply against the attack, the attack bypasses only one—whichever would result in the highest amount of damage getting through, as determined by the GM.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Auspicious Scepter

</row>

</column>

---

This vivid red cloak of sturdy fabric is favored by aggressive battle alchemists and mages, as well as those cautious warriors who need to advance into the fray ahead of the blast-happy back line. The mantle interposes to protect you from your own and allies' magic, granting you a circumstance bonus to Reflex saves against your own spells, as well as those of your allies. You also gain resistance to [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage from your own alchemical items and those of your allies.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bane](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1041)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 180

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _bane_ rune causes a weapon to grant you improved understanding of creatures of a particular type, allowing you to deal more damage to those creatures. The crafter chooses [aberration](/Traits.aspx?ID=1), [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9), [beast](/Traits.aspx?ID=20), [celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=23), [construct](/Traits.aspx?ID=35), [dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=50), [elemental](/Traits.aspx?ID=57), [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69), [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=70), [giant](/Traits.aspx?ID=79), [monitor](/Traits.aspx?ID=111), [ooze](/Traits.aspx?ID=122), or both [fungus](/Traits.aspx?ID=77) and [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125). The weapon deals 1d6 additional damage of the weapon's damage type to creatures with the chosen trait or traits. The benefit doesn't apply against creatures of the chosen type disguised as other creatures. It's up to GM discretion whether the _bane_ rune applies against a creature disguised as a creature of the chosen type.

 The GM might allow _bane_ runes for other creature traits, such as [astral](/Traits.aspx?ID=14), [dream](/Traits.aspx?ID=299), or [demon](/Traits.aspx?ID=42). However, [humanoids](/Traits.aspx?ID=91), [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160), and specific types of humanoids (such as [elves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=2)) are never a valid option.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bane

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blade of Four Energies](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1042)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 180

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [shifting](/Equipment.aspx?ID=302) [shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43)_ is formed of rapidly vibrating air and magical energy, though it uses the same statistics as iron. The wooden hilt is adorned with four gems, representing the energies of acid, cold, fire, and electricity, that sparkle in sequence at random intervals.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per round;   **Effect** You draw magical energy from one of the gems. Roll 1d4. Until the start of your next turn, the weapon gains the corresponding rune: 1—[_corrosive_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=292), 2—[_flaming_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295), 3—[_frost_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=296), or 4—[_shock_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=303).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** The blade has an energy rune from the first activation; **Effect** You call upon the energy stored in the blade to explode outward. The blade of four energies casts a 5th-level spell that depends on the active rune: [_acid arrow_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2) for _corrosive_, [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=119) for _flaming_, [_cone of cold_](/Spells.aspx?ID=47) for _frost_, or [_lightning bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=172) for _shock_, with a DC of 30 and spell attack bonus of +20.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

</traits>

</column>

Blade of Four Energies (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The blade is a _+3 greater striking shifting shortsword_ and gains the _greater corrosive_, _greater flaming_, _greater frost_, or _greater shock_ runes instead of their base versions. When Activated to Cast a Spell, the blade casts the spell heightened to 7th level, and the DC is 38.
name: Blade of Four Energies

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blade of Four Energies](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1042)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 180

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [shifting](/Equipment.aspx?ID=302) [shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43)_ is formed of rapidly vibrating air and magical energy, though it uses the same statistics as iron. The wooden hilt is adorned with four gems, representing the energies of acid, cold, fire, and electricity, that sparkle in sequence at random intervals.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per round;   **Effect** You draw magical energy from one of the gems. Roll 1d4. Until the start of your next turn, the weapon gains the corresponding rune: 1—[_corrosive_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=292), 2—[_flaming_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295), 3—[_frost_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=296), or 4—[_shock_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=303).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** The blade has an energy rune from the first activation; **Effect** You call upon the energy stored in the blade to explode outward. The blade of four energies casts a 5th-level spell that depends on the active rune: [_acid arrow_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2) for _corrosive_, [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=119) for _flaming_, [_cone of cold_](/Spells.aspx?ID=47) for _frost_, or [_lightning bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=172) for _shock_, with a DC of 30 and spell attack bonus of +20.

name: Blade of Four Energies (Greater)

This rune causes a weapon to transform into pure, brilliant energy. The weapon deals an additional 1d4 fire damage on a successful Strike, as well as 1d4 good damage to [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=70) and 1d4 positive damage to [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160). On a critical hit, the target must succeed at a DC 29 Fortitude save or be [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 round.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command ([light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100))    **Effect** You plunge your weapon into darkness to return the light. Attempt a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check with a counteract level of 5 and a +19 counteract modifier to end a magical [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39) effect whose area is within reach of the weapon.

    level="1"
    right="Item 11+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Buzzsaw Axe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1045)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 181

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Battle Axe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=16)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

With an aerodynamic cutting edge and a curved handle, this _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [battle axe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=16)_ is perfectly suited to whirling motions, and in fact seems like it wants to whirl free from your grip. A Strike with this axe that benefits from the [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194) trait's circumstance bonus on attack rolls also gains a +2 circumstance bonus to the damage roll.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You hurl the axe, which spins at great speed as it flies to a distant point and returns to you, leaving a trail of carnage. Each creature in a 120-foot line must succeed at a DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save or take damage equal to the weapon's melee damage, including your Strength modifier.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

The axe is a _+3 major striking battle axe_, the circumstance bonus to damage is +4, and the activation's DC is 43.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cantrip Deck](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1046)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 181

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

In an effort to spread the knowledge of magic as widely as possible, worshippers of [Nethys](/Deities.aspx?ID=12) discovered a way to bind cantrips into cards accessible even to non-spellcasters. The deck contains thick parchment cards, each roughly half the size of a playing card. In precise, no-nonsense script, each card simply states the name of its cantrip, color-coded based on its school.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action or more Actions" /> (envision, Interact);     **Effect** You envision your desired cantrip, causing its card to rise to the top of the deck, and draw the card. The deck casts that cantrip as a 1st-level spell, with a DC of 15 and a spell attack modifier of +5. The card crumbles into dust as the cantrip takes effect. The activation takes the same number of actions as the cantrip you chose takes to cast.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Cantrip Deck (5-Pack)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 181

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This pack contains 5 cards of a single common cantrip (such as [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=171)).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Cantrip Deck (Full Pack)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 181

**Price** 20 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This pack contains 24 cards—one of each cantrip in the _Core Rulebook_.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cantrip Deck](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1046)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 181

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

In an effort to spread the knowledge of magic as widely as possible, worshippers of [Nethys](/Deities.aspx?ID=12) discovered a way to bind cantrips into cards accessible even to non-spellcasters. The deck contains thick parchment cards, each roughly half the size of a playing card. In precise, no-nonsense script, each card simply states the name of its cantrip, color-coded based on its school.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action or more Actions" /> (envision, Interact);     **Effect** You envision your desired cantrip, causing its card to rise to the top of the deck, and draw the card. The deck casts that cantrip as a 1st-level spell, with a DC of 15 and a spell attack modifier of +5. The card crumbles into dust as the cantrip takes effect. The activation takes the same number of actions as the cantrip you chose takes to cast.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Chatterer of Follies](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1047)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 181

**Price** 200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Khakkara](/Weapons.aspx?ID=126)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This heavy _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [khakkara](/Weapons.aspx?ID=126)_ is cast from solid steel and plated with pyrite. Metal charms shaped like grinning idols festoon its great arched ring, and during combat, as tiny tendrils of flame fly off the pyrite, these hanging dolls seem to chatter in tune with the clash and din of battle.

 Whenever you complete a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) action during your turn, choose a creature adjacent to you at the end of your movement. Until the end of your turn, that creature takes a –1 status penalty to Will saves against your [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61) spells and your [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92) spells. Any time you cast your enchantment and illusion spells, you become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) during your move actions for the rest of the turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Chatterer of Follies

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cloak of Feline Rest](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1048)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 182

**Price** 20 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This black velvet cloak is featureless and very soft to the touch. Upon wearing it for the first time, you're momentarily overwhelmed with a sense of comfort and coziness. While wearing this cloak you can comfortably rest in any space, so long as it's not wet or particularly hazardous. While sleeping in this cloak you only take a –2 status penalty to [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) Perception checks, rather than a –4 status penalty.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Cloak of Feline Rest

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Codebreaker's Parchment](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1049)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 182

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This finely crafted, seemingly mundane parchment is useful for writing sensitive documents. When words are written on this parchment, they instantly scramble into unrecognizable script, requiring a DC 20 check to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=22).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;     **Effect** The scrambled words align into the originally written script. Identifying the parchment reveals that it can be activated with a command, but a critical success to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24) is needed to learn the command word.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You light the parchment on fire, burning the words off while leaving the parchment unharmed and ready to bear more text.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Collar of the Eternal Bond](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1050)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Eidolon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=386" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 182

**Price** 330 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn collar

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you invest this collar for your eidolon, it changes its appearance to match the eidolon's form, possibly into a different sort of neckpiece such as a choker, and it glows brightly with the symbol you and your eidolon share. While your eidolon wears a _collar of the eternal bond_, the collar bolsters their connection to you, allowing them to move up to 150 feet from you before unmanifesting instead of 100 feet. The eidolon can also Activate the collar to move even further.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Your eidolon focuses their will on the collar, allowing the collar to maintain the connection between you at any distance. For the next 5 minutes, you and your eidolon can move any distance from each other without your eidolon unmanifesting. When the duration ends, if your eidolon is more than 150 feet from you, they immediately unmanifest.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Collar of the Eternal Bond

    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Conflagration Club](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1051)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 182

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Greatclub](/Weapons.aspx?ID=22)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A ring of magical crystals encircles the base of this _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [greatclub](/Weapons.aspx?ID=22)_, allowing it to absorb and store magical energy.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;   **Trigger** You Cast a Spell from a spell slot, and the spell has the [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147) trait; **Requirements** The club has no charges; **Effect** You mentally align the club's crystals to reverberate with energy from the spell. It gains a number of charges equal to the level of the spell slot, and the charges are attuned to the damage type matching the spell's trait. If the spell has more than one eligible trait, choose one when the charges are stored.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103))   **Requirements** The club has at least 1 charge; **Effect** You flip the club around to blast a cone of energy from its base. Each creature in a 15-foot cone takes 1d6 damage per charge stored in the club, with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save against your spell DC or DC 19, whichever is greater. The damage type is the same type the charges were attuned to. The club loses all of its charges.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Conflagration Club

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 182 **Price** 200 gp **Usage** held in 2 hands --- This set of 34 parchment cards usually comes in a velvet bag or simple leather wrap. Each card depicts a different creature, monster, or other being that, when the deck is activated, immediately appears as a believable, life-size illusion. You can look at the card's artwork, but no magical effect takes place until you Activate the deck, shuffling and drawing randomly. **Activate** envision, Interact; **Effect** You draw a card, chosen randomly from the remaining cards in the deck, and throw it to the ground to create an illusion of the creature depicted. The image is an [_illusory creature_](/Spells.aspx?ID=158), except it has a range of only 30 feet from where the card was thrown and the illusion lasts until destroyed or until anyone moves or damages the card. The creature takes its actions once on your turn if you're within 60 feet of the card and Sustain the Activation. The illusion ends if you don't Sustain it, or if you activate a new card from the deck. Deck Of Illusions Cards You can use playing cards or Tarot cards as proxies for the cards in a deck of illusions.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Deck of Mischief](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1053)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 183

**Price** 75 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This deck of 54 cards appears nearly identical to standard [playing cards](/Equipment.aspx?ID=873). Comprised of four thematic suits of 13 cards each, as well as two wildcards, the _deck of mischief_ is a favorite of scoundrels who prefer not to leave their games to chance—or to at least nudge the odds in their favor. If you know how to activate the deck, you can illusorily transform the ace and face cards into other cards in the deck.

 The deck is true to its name and less likely to comply with your wishes if not well cared for. While surprisingly resistant to the elements, a waterlogged, dirty, or battered deck may not function as desired, giving you incorrect information about which cards are still in the deck or failing to hold the illusion at a critical moment. If a deck becomes incomplete due to damaged or missing cards, it rapidly loses its deceptive capabilities and might even purposefully sabotage you.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision, Interact;     **Effect** You learn which of the ace and face cards are still in the deck. You can then swap the apparent face of an ace or face card in your hand (if you have any) with the face of one still in the deck. A creature who [Seeks](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) or touches the card can attempt to disbelieve this illusion (Perception DC 20).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Deck of Mischief

---

This small, heart-shaped amulet appears to be sculpted from stone with a single streak of dim light running through, like an ember just beneath ashes. When worn, the amulet gives off a gentle warmth, akin to being near a fireplace. You gain [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) resistance 15 when wearing this amulet.

 When you invest the amulet, you either increase your Constitution score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You hold the amulet aloft as a ripple of warm orange light exudes outward. Each ally in a 30-foot emanation regains 30 Hit Points and gains a +3 status bonus to Fortitude saves until the end of their next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

[Flask of Fellowship](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1056)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 184

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This is a metal drinking flask, 4 inches in diameter and 10 inches tall. Its screw top is covered by four simple metal cups that nest together.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Make an Impression;     **Effect** If you share drinks from a _flask of fellowship_ as part of your Make an Impression action, the drink that pours from the flask happens to be exactly what the target of your efforts would most like to have a dram or two of—wine, spirits, hot ginger tea, or ice cold water with lemon, for example. You gain a +1 item bonus on your [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) check. 

The GM can disallow the flask's use if it doesn't make sense in the moment for you to pull out a flask and start pouring, if drinks are inappropriate for the occasion, or if your target is absolutely not interested in sharing a drink with you. Drinks from the _flask of fellowship_ are entirely social; they won't intoxicate anyone, nor alleviate serious thirst.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Flask of Fellowship

These wire-rim glasses with large, circular lenses were invented for the sole purpose of avoiding awkward confrontations at social gatherings. They grant you a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;     **Effect** You stare at another creature and instantly remember their name if you've met and exchanged names. The glasses rely on your latent memories, so if the creature is disguising their identity, the glasses don't penetrate the disguise. If a [doppelganger](/Monsters.aspx?ID=126) was disguised as an innkeeper you met, the glasses would give you the innkeeper's name, and if a noble you met before was in disguise as a masked vigilante, the glasses wouldn't reveal their name.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

This _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301) [starknife](/Weapons.aspx?ID=44)_ is made of dark blue metal overlaid with smoky flecks of fused quartz. When you Cast an [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65) Spell, your next attack this turn with this starknife reduces the target's cover, changing greater cover to standard cover or ignoring standard and lesser cover.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Trigger** You successfully Strike a creature with a [thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=195) Strike with _guiding star_;  **Effect** Soft light outlines the target. At the beginning of your next turn, the _guiding star's_ blades detach and form into darts of starlight, flying up to 40 feet and striking the target unerringly, collectively dealing 4 piercing damage and 1 good damage before re-forming. The piercing damage increases to 6 with a _greater striking rune_ and 8 with a _major striking rune_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Guiding Star

[Hat of Many Minds](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1060)
</title>

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

</row>

</column>

---

This pointy, brimmed hat made up of a rainbow patchwork of various materials seems to sit just a little lopsided on your head, no matter how you adjust it. You gain a +2 item bonus to checks to [Earn Income](/Actions.aspx?ID=23).

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You tear off a patch of cloth to manifest it into a copy of yourself, dressed in the color and fabric of the patch. The copy follows your specific instructions and performs a single, straightforward task for up to 30 minutes. It takes the copy three times as long to complete the task as it would you, meaning it can perform a task that would take you a maximum of 10 minutes. It doesn't react quickly enough to be of any use during an encounter, and it can't use your spells or other special abilities—just basic actions and skill actions.

 The copy disappears and returns to the hat as a piece of cloth when the given task has been completed, or when you Dismiss the activation.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hat of Many Minds

---

This _+2 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [invisibility](/Equipment.aspx?ID=287) [chain shirt](/Armor.aspx?ID=6)_ is itself invisible. Other creatures can't see it at all, allowing you to wear it surreptitiously. Additionally, the armor's invisible composition is quieter and more comfortable than a normal chain shirt. It loses the [noisy](/Traits.aspx?ID=205) trait and gains the [comfort](/Traits.aspx?ID=203) trait.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** You Stride and turn partially invisible, becoming [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) until the end of your next turn or until you use a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300), whichever comes first. As normal, you can use the concealment from this activation to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

These [thieves' tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=58) provide their owner the ability to have continued control over a lock while leaving it in place. They grant a +1 item bonus to [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) checks to [Pick a Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=70). Upon completely opening a lock by picking it with these tools, the tools produce a temporary copy of the key for the picked lock. This phantom key can lock or unlock the lock just like the original key. The key appears attached to the thieves' tools case by a fine silver chain and lasts for 12 hours before it fades into nothing. Only one key created this way can exist in the same set of thieves' tools. Creating a new key replaces the previous one.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Keymaking Tools

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 186 **Price** 24,000 gp **Usage** worn mask **Bulk** L --- This mask appears to be a pool of mirrored, shifting silver adhered to a thin metal plate. When you place it against your face, it melds to the shape of your head. The material is breathable and light, and does not obscure vision. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5), [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6), [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7), and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks while wearing the mask. When you invest the mask, you either increase your Charisma score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score. **Activate** envision ([visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You attempt a Deception, Diplomacy, Intimidation, or Performance check; **Effect** You gain a +4 status bonus to the triggering check. This ability has no effect if you're under the effects of a disguise that hides the _mask of allure_. Depending on the skill used, the mirrored silver transforms into one of the following appearances.
  • **Deception** A swirl of silver, which entirely obscures and conceals your normal features.
  • **Diplomacy** A kind, gentle and inviting expression.
  • **Intimidation** A monstrous visage that shocks viewers into paying attention.
  • **Performance** A jovial humanoid face, the mouth cracked wide with a comedic smile or a tragic frown, depending on the nature of the performance.
name: Mask of Allure

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 187 **Price** 24,000 gp **Usage** worn cloak **Bulk** L --- This deep red cloak fits lightly about your shoulders, and the edges perpetually twitch slightly, as though caught in a breeze. The cloth feels smoother than silk, rippling and swaying like liquid when in motion. You feel a lively energy infusing your arms and legs. You gain a +3 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) and [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15), and a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against attacks from reactions triggered by your movement. When you invest the cloak, you either increase your Dexterity score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score. **Activate** Interact; **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** An enemy misses you with a melee Strike; **Effect** You slip around the attacking creature with ease. You Step, without moving away from the triggering enemy, and then make a melee Strike against the triggering enemy if it's within reach. If you do make a Strike, the target attempts a DC 38 Perception check before you roll. **Failure** This creature is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against the Strike.
**Critical Failure** This creature is flat-footed against all your attacks until the end of their next turn. **Activate** command, envision ([conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The cloak hums with power as your whirl it around yourself, disappearing amid a brief flash of light. Teleport up to double your Speed to a location you can see. At the end of the teleportation, you can make a melee Strike against a creature within reach, if there is one.
name: Mercurial Mantle

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[North Wind's Night Verse](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1068)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=27" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 187

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Katana](/Weapons.aspx?ID=55)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [frost](/Equipment.aspx?ID=296) [katana](/Weapons.aspx?ID=55)_ is always cool to the touch, nearly uncomfortably so. Unadorned and with no tsuba, its honed blade is carved from a single tusk of [dragon-turtle](/Monsters.aspx?ID=157) ivory and wrapped in strips of [winter wolf](/Monsters.aspx?ID=403) hide. Strikes with the katana gain a +2 status bonus to damage rolls against creatures that have a status penalty to their Speed or are [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35). The status bonus increases to +3 if the weapon has a _greater striking rune_ and +4 for _major striking_.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> command ([cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) **Frequency** once per 1 minute; **Trigger** Your spell deals cold damage to a creature;  **Effect** Choose one creature dealt cold damage by the spell. It takes a –5-foot status penalty to its Speeds for 1 round.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: North Wind's Night Verse

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ouroboros Flail](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1069)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 187

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [War Flail](/Weapons.aspx?ID=46)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You can easily expand and contract the chain of this _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [extending](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1055) [war flail](/Weapons.aspx?ID=46)_. It magically grows new links when extended and loses them when contracted.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> command;  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You successfully Strike with the flail while activating its _extending_ rune;  **Effect** The head of the flail wraps around the target of the triggering Strike and drags it in your direction. The target must succeed at a DC 31 Fortitude save or be [pulled](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) adjacent to you. A creature pulled in is then [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by the flail until the end of your next turn unless you move, your target [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (DC 31), or you use the flail.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> Interact;     **Effect** You extend the flail's chain, then wrap it across itself and strike two links together. This severs a usable 60-foot loop of chain from the flail, which instantly returns to its normal length. If you use this activation again, any previous chain you created with it disappears after 1 round. As with most obviously magical and temporary constructs, the chain cannot be sold.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rebounding Breastplate](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1070)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Force" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=75" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 188

**Price** 70,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+3 [greater resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [greater fortification](/Equipment.aspx?ID=285) [breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10)_ absorbs impacts for later release. While wearing the armor, you gain resistance 5 to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage and resistance 10 to [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75) damage. Keep track of how much damage the armor prevents from enemy attacks, as the armor absorbs that damage. After 1 minute, the absorbed damage disperses harmlessly and resets to 0. Only damage caused by foes or hazards powers the armor, not damage you take from yourself, allies, or the environment.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;    **Requirements** Your armor has absorbed at least 30 damage; **Effect** The absorbed energy explodes from your armor in a pulse of force. Creatures in a 30-foot emanation take 10d6 force damage with a DC 43 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. On a failure, they're [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet away from you, and on a critical failure, they're pushed 20 feet away from you and fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). The armor's absorbed damage resets to 0.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Rebounding Breastplate

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Restful Tent](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1071)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 188

**Price** 320 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This four-person tent provides several benefits for those who rest within. The climate inside the tent is comfortable and allows creatures inside it to withstand most hostile weather conditions, but incredible heat or cold, powerful storms, and winds of hurricane force or greater can still damage or destroy the tent. Mundane pests such as solitary ordinary insects avoid the tent, though swarms and giant insects can attack the tent as normal. Once you pitch the tent, only you can easily move it; any other creatures must succeed at a DC 20 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to do so. Finally, the tent automatically camouflages with its surroundings, requiring a [Searching](/Actions.aspx?ID=519) creature to succeed at a DC 22 Perception check to notice it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Restful Tent

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 189 **Price** 24,000 gp **Usage** worn belt **Bulk** 1 --- The thin, glittering strands of rope that comprise this thick belt appear to be spun gold. Strung along the front of the belt are a collection of four multicolored, perfectly spherical beads: jade, turquoise, quartz, and amethyst. While wearing the lash, you gain a +3 item bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13). When you invest the belt, you either increase your Wisdom score by 2 or to 18, whichever would give you a higher score. **Activate** command, Interact; **Effect** You touch one of the jewels affixed to the sage's lash and speak a command word. Depending on the jewel, a different effect is produced that affects you and all living creatures in a 30-foot emanation. After the effect occurs, all four jewels disappear from the lash, reappearing at the next dawn.
  • **Jade** ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128)) Speak “purity” to produce a wave of green light that ends any [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) condition and any [ongoing poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) affecting creatures in the emanation.
  • **Turquoise** Speak “clarity” to grant all affected a +2 status bonus to their next Will save for 1 minute.
  • **Quartz** ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128)) Speak “lenity” to have each creature regain 30 Hit Points.
  • **Amethyst** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), [revelation](/Traits.aspx?ID=138)) Speak “verity” to emit a magical trail of light from the lash that reveals the location of each creature in the emanation. For 1 round, if they would be [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26), they become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) instead, and if they would be [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) or [unnoticed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=41) by a creature, they're [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) instead. A creature can attempt a DC 43 [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) check to avoid the lash's detection.
name: Sage's Lash

When you shoot the bow, it releases a soft musical note—sonorous if your aim is true and discordant if your shot goes off-target. **Activate** command ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** You sing, hum, or whistle a note, and your bow provides appropriate accompaniment. Make a Strike with the bow. If it hits, the bow generates a magical effect determined by the note you sung, which has the traits indicated in parentheses. This affects every creature in a 10-foot emanation from the creature you hit. As normal for an emanation, you choose whether the creature you hit is affected.
  • **Song of Soothing** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) Each creature regains 3d10 Hit Points and gains a +1 status bonus to saves against [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects for 1 minute.
  • **Song of Suffering** ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) Each creature takes 3d10 sonic damage with a DC 31 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />

---

This _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295) [scimitar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=38)_ is always warm to the touch, nearly unbearably so. Lines of crackling flame radiate from carnelians affixed to its curved and blackened blade, and its pommel ends in a brilliant tassel of [phoenix](/Monsters.aspx?ID=330) feathers. While you have a status bonus to at least one of your Speeds, your Strikes with this scimitar that deal fire damage gain a +2 status bonus to their fire damage. The status bonus increases to +3 if the weapon has a _greater striking_ rune and +4 for _major striking_.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> command ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103))  **Trigger** Your spell deals fire damage to a creature;  **Effect** You gain a +10-foot status bonus to your Speed until the end of your next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: South Wind's Scorch Song

---

Each _specialist's ring_ is dedicated to a single school of magic, and the ring is covered in symbols and glyphs related to that school according to the creator's arcane studies. A _specialist's ring_ has the trait corresponding to its school of magic. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) checks, and a +1 circumstance bonus to recognize magical effects and items of the specific school of magic.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast a wizard school spell of the corresponding school. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Specialist's Ring

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 190 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- This crooked staff is made from twisting acacia wood and has a sphere of rough sandstone embedded in the top, to channel the magic of the desert. When wielding this staff in deserts, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) checks to [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=27), [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=66), and [Cover Tracks](/Actions.aspx?ID=65). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells Staff of the Desert Winds **Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 190 **Price** 230 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **Cantrip** [_know direction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=169)
  • **1st** [_create water_](/Spells.aspx?ID=53), [_pass without trace_](/Spells.aspx?ID=215)
  • **2nd** [_endure elements_](/Spells.aspx?ID=99), [_enhance victuals_](/Spells.aspx?ID=101)
Staff of the Desert Winds (Greater) **Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 190 **Price** 1,750 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **3rd** [_remove disease_](/Spells.aspx?ID=251), [_wall of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=367)
  • **4th** [_air walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=6)
  • **5th** [_elemental form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=98)
Staff of the Desert Winds (Major) **Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 190 **Price** 10,000 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **6rd** [_flesh to stone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=123), [_stone to flesh_](/Spells.aspx?ID=311)
  • **7th** [_fiery body_](/Spells.aspx?ID=115)
Staff of the Desert Winds (True) **Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 190 **Price** 70,000 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **8rd** [_horrid wilting_](/Spells.aspx?ID=152), [_wind walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=376)
  • **9th** [_storm of vengeance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=313)
name: Staff of the Desert Winds

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Staff-Storing Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1079)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 190

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Wooden Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=2)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This magically reinforced [wooden shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=2) (Hardness 6, HP 36, BT 18) normally has a blank face. It can absorb a [staff](/Equipment.aspx?Category=32) and transform between a shield and staff. When you prepare a staff, you can hold it up to the shield, at which point the items will merge, and the shield's face becomes an image corresponding to the type of magic, such as a skull for a [_staff of necromancy_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=360).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You change the _staff-storing shield_ from its shield form into the stored staff, which has an image of the shield on it, or change it from the stored staff back into a shield.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Staff-Storing Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1079)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 190

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Wooden Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=2)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This magically reinforced [wooden shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=2) (Hardness 6, HP 36, BT 18) normally has a blank face. It can absorb a [staff](/Equipment.aspx?Category=32) and transform between a shield and staff. When you prepare a staff, you can hold it up to the shield, at which point the items will merge, and the shield's face becomes an image corresponding to the type of magic, such as a skull for a [_staff of necromancy_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=360).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You change the _staff-storing shield_ from its shield form into the stored staff, which has an image of the shield on it, or change it from the stored staff back into a shield.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Stampede Medallion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1080)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Eidolon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=386" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 190

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you invest this medallion for your eidolon, it changes shape to appear as a tiny bejeweled facsimile of the eidolon, magically attached just over your eidolon's heart. While your eidolon wears the medallion, they gain a +2 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Your eidolon Concentrates on the medallion and their connection to you, allowing them to momentarily manifest into a stampede of dozens of copies of themself. The stampede rampages out in every direction, swerving around your allies while trampling any foe on the ground in an emanation around your eidolon with a radius equal to your eidolon's Speed. Each of these foes takes 8d6 bludgeoning damage, with a DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. On a critical failure, the foe is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). After dealing damage, the stampede of eidolons vanishes as quickly as it appeared.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Stampede Medallion
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 190

**Price** 1,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 15"
pfs="Standard"
>
Stampede Medallion (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 190

**Price** 6,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The activation's DC is 34 and the damage is 12d6.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Stampede Medallion (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 190

**Price** 21,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +3, the activation's DC is 38 and the damage is 14d6.
name: Stampede Medallion

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Thousand-Blade Thesis](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1081)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 191

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This collection of lacquered rice paper scrolls mounted on flexible bamboo contains a wealth of calligraphic essays and paintings on the art of war, specifically focused on the use of various weapons in warfare and how to tactically deploy warriors using those weapons to the best possible advantage. Consulting the thesis grants a +2 item bonus to [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) checks. Like most scholarly compendiums, this usage requires holding the thesis in one hand.

 The thesis also serves as an extradimensional armory for weapons and ammunition. The thesis has a capacity of 5 Bulk, and only weapons and ammunition can be stored within it. You and others can Interact with the thesis to store or retrieve a weapon or piece of ammunition in it, like a mundane container.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _thousand-blade thesis_ dramatically unfurls, and the weapons contained within it spring forth and array themselves impressively in the air, floating within easy reach. For 1 minute, you can use a free action to Interact to draw one of the floating weapons. Others can attempt to nab them out of the air, but to do so they must critically succeed at a [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) check. You can't place weapons back into the thesis until the minute elapses.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Thousand-Blade Thesis

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 191 **Price** 24,000 gp **Usage** worn gloves **Bulk** L --- These bronze gauntlets each have a small red gem embedded in the wrist. You gain a +3 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks and a +1 circumstance bonus to Athletics checks to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35). If you successfully Grapple an enemy that's at least one size category larger than you, the gauntlets dig into it, dealing bludgeoning damage equal to your Strength modifier, plus an additional 2d6 on a critical success. When you invest the gloves, you either increase your Strength score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score. **Activate** Interact ([sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** You have two hands free; **Effect** You clap the gauntlets together with a thunderous crack that deals 6d10 sonic damage in a 30-foot emanation. Each creature in the area must attempt a DC 35 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage, is deafened for 1 minute, and is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).
name: Titan's Grasp

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[True Name Amulet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1083)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 247

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn amulet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This amulet bears the true name of a single creature with a level no higher than the item's level. These amulets are typically made of gold and engraved, but could be made of anything, including simple clay. The name is clearly visible, though only to you, and only while you have the amulet invested.

If you can read the language in which the name is written, that creature always takes the effects of [_invoke true name_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1065) against your spells (with a spell level equal to half the item's level, rounded up). In addition, when the creature uses any magical effect against you, you can immediately attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) it using [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Domains.aspx?ID=22), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) for the counteract check and the _true name amulet's_ level to determine the counteract level. Legendary amulets bearing the true names of creatures of level 21+ may exist.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
