markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 183 **Price** 5 sp **Hands** 2 --- Also known as the Magician's Deck in Taldor or the Deck of Masks in the Shackles, the non-magical deck of cards called the Old Mage Deck exists with minor variances across Golarion. The four suits correspond to the four essences of magic (matter, spirit, mind, life) and each contains 13 numbered cards with characters depicted on them, plus two wildcards. While common decks are inexpensive (costing 5 sp), limited-edition decks with elaborate art and high-quality materials are often highly sought after by collectors. A deck has negligible Bulk, and takes both hands to use. In a classic Old Mage Deck, the 13 cards in each suit are as follows, with Jatembe's Ten Magic Warriors as cards 2 through 11: Initiate (1), Red Hyena (2), Grey Elephant (3), Golden Snake (4), Black Ibex (5), Frog of Shifting Colors (6), Emerald Spider (7), Walnut Hawk (8), White Bull (9), Blue Leopard (10), Black Heron (11), a different creature for each suit representing the given suit's magical essence (12), and Old Mage Jatembe using magic of the suit's essence (13). Wildcards vary from deck to deck, making each Old Mage Deck unique. Old Mage Deck Substitutions
**Old Mage Deck****Standard Deck****Minor Arcana**
Suit of MatterDiamondsPentacles
Suit of SpiritClubsWands
Suit of MindSpadesSwords
Suit of LifeHeartsCups
OneAceAce
ElevenJackKnight
TwelveQueenQueen
ThirteenKingKing
WildcardsJokersAny two Pages or Major Arcana cards
name: The Old Mage Deck

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

</traits>

**Source** [Pathfinder #170: Spoken on the Song Wind](/Sources.aspx?ID=97) pg. 75

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This beautiful leather bag is lined in soft fur and purrs quietly when pet.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You shake the bag to produce a single cat. You cast a 3rd-level [_summon animal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=316) to produce a [leopard](/Monsters.aspx?ID=67).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You pull the bag over your head and it covers you completely. You then exit the bag in the form of a large cat. You gain the effects of 3rd-level [_animal form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=10) but must use the cat battle form.

</column>

>
[Crown of the Companion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1086)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Positive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=128" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #170: Spoken on the Song Wind](/Sources.aspx?ID=97) pg. 75, [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 151

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn crown

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Stories tell of a forgotten king who once loved his subjects so much he was willing to give his own life energy for them. Whether true or not, this majestic wooden crown bears elaborate carvings depicting that tale. It's ringed with images of the same kingly figure giving more and more of himself to a throng of needy subjects. While wearing it, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You bow to an ally within 30 feet, creating a magical bond between the two of you. You cast [_shield other_](/Spells.aspx?ID=281) targeting the ally. The link remains even if you move more than 30 feet away from them. At the end of the spell's duration, your ally recovers 4d8 Hit Points and you recover half of what they recover.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Crown of the Companion

This _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301) [spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10)_ is warm to the touch. The head of the spear is crafted from gold with ivory inlay and has a ruby set into the shaft. You can make the spear glow like a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=60) or suppress its light by using an action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You touch the ruby and hurl the spear at a square within 40 feet. When the spear lands, it detonates, dealing 6d6 fire damage (DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save) to all creatures in a 10-foot emanation. It then flies back to your hand.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gesture at a square within 40 feet with _Habu's Cudgel_. The club causes a frightful wail to unleash from that square and casts [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=110) (DC 24 Will save) targeting the creature in that square and all adjacent creatures.

</title>

<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />

Made of cobalt-colored steel rings that flow down the entire length of the garment, this _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [chain mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=9)_ includes an armored face covering. The armor is said to be based on the same armor once worn by Azure Leopard, one of Old-Mage Jatembe's magic warriors.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You call upon the ferocity and grace of the leopard. The armor's rings flatten out to become a solid piece of flexible armor. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks and [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53), but take a –2 penalty to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) and [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) checks. This lasts for 1 hour, but you can Activate the armor again to revert it back to its original form.

</column>

>
[Warcaller's Chime](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1093)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The Nantambu Chime-Ringers created these magical chimes to aid in their defense of their city. Each chime is made from elaborately carved wood depicting the nature of a given chime's power.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision;     **Effect** You ring the chime, unleashing its magic. The specifics of each chime, as well as the activation's frequency (if any), appear in its entry below.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Limited"
>
Warcaller's Chime of Blasting
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #170: Spoken on the Song Wind](/Sources.aspx?ID=97) pg. 77

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

This chime features carvings of destructive runes. When activated, it creates the effects of a 3rd-level [_sound burst_](/Spells.aspx?ID=292) centered on you (DC 20 Fortitude save). Instead of the spell's normal range, it has a 5-foot emanation. In addition, creatures that critically fail their save are knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) by the chime's effect. You can use this chime once per day.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Limited"
>
Warcaller's Chime of Destruction
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #170: Spoken on the Song Wind](/Sources.aspx?ID=97) pg. 77

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

A series of shattered mountains are carved into this chime. When activated, you touch the chime against an unattended object, casting [_shatter_](/Spells.aspx?ID=279) upon the object, ignoring the object's Hardness if it's 6 or lower. You can use this chime once per day.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Limited"
>
Warcaller's Chime of Dread
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #170: Spoken on the Song Wind](/Sources.aspx?ID=97) pg. 77

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

This chime's carvings depict skulls and other grim icons. When activated, it creates an aura of fear in a 5-foot emanation for 1 round. Creatures in the aura must attempt a DC 27 Will save. On a failure, they become [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) (frightened 2 on a critical failure). Affected creatures can't reduce their frightened condition below frightened 1 while the chime is ringing. You can Sustain the Activation to sustain the aura, up to a total of 5 rounds. This chime can be used only once per day, whether or not you Sustain the aura.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Limited"
>
Warcaller's Chime of Refuge
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #170: Spoken on the Song Wind](/Sources.aspx?ID=97) pg. 77

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

This chime's carvings depict a series of warding runes that evoke feelings of serene protection. When activated, you touch the chime to yourself or a willing creature. The target gains the effects of [_sanctuary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=266) (DC 20 Will save). You can use this chime up to five times per day, but each use must target a different creature.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Limited"
>
Warcaller's Chime of Resistance
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #170: Spoken on the Song Wind](/Sources.aspx?ID=97) pg. 77

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

This chime features several elemental runes in its carvings. When activated, it creates a defensive aura in a 5-foot emanation. As you activate the chime, choose acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic. You and creatures in the aura gain resistance 5 against the damage type you chose. This aura remains active for 10 minutes. This chime can be used once per week.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Limited"
>
Warcaller's Chime of Restoration
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #170: Spoken on the Song Wind](/Sources.aspx?ID=97) pg. 77

**Price** 2,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

This chime's carvings depict blooming flowers. When activated, it creates a small aura of healing in a 5-foot emanation. Creatures in the aura gain [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) 15. This aura remains active for 3 rounds. This chime can be used once per week.
name: Warcaller's Chime

---

The Nantambu Chime-Ringers created these magical chimes to aid in their defense of their city. Each chime is made from elaborately carved wood depicting the nature of a given chime's power.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision;     **Effect** You ring the chime, unleashing its magic. The specifics of each chime, as well as the activation's frequency (if any), appear in its entry below.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Limited"
>
Warcaller's Chime of Blasting
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #170: Spoken on the Song Wind](/Sources.aspx?ID=97) pg. 77

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

This chime features carvings of destructive runes. When activated, it creates the effects of a 3rd-level [_sound burst_](/Spells.aspx?ID=292) centered on you (DC 20 Fortitude save). Instead of the spell's normal range, it has a 5-foot emanation. In addition, creatures that critically fail their save are knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) by the chime's effect. You can use this chime once per day.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Limited"
>
Warcaller's Chime of Destruction
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #170: Spoken on the Song Wind](/Sources.aspx?ID=97) pg. 77

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

A series of shattered mountains are carved into this chime. When activated, you touch the chime against an unattended object, casting [_shatter_](/Spells.aspx?ID=279) upon the object, ignoring the object's Hardness if it's 6 or lower. You can use this chime once per day.

This chime's carvings depict skulls and other grim icons. When activated, it creates an aura of fear in a 5-foot emanation for 1 round. Creatures in the aura must attempt a DC 27 Will save. On a failure, they become [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) (frightened 2 on a critical failure). Affected creatures can't reduce their frightened condition below frightened 1 while the chime is ringing. You can Sustain the Activation to sustain the aura, up to a total of 5 rounds. This chime can be used only once per day, whether or not you Sustain the aura.

The Nantambu Chime-Ringers created these magical chimes to aid in their defense of their city. Each chime is made from elaborately carved wood depicting the nature of a given chime's power.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision;     **Effect** You ring the chime, unleashing its magic. The specifics of each chime, as well as the activation's frequency (if any), appear in its entry below.

---

This chime features carvings of destructive runes. When activated, it creates the effects of a 3rd-level [_sound burst_](/Spells.aspx?ID=292) centered on you (DC 20 Fortitude save). Instead of the spell's normal range, it has a 5-foot emanation. In addition, creatures that critically fail their save are knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) by the chime's effect. You can use this chime once per day.

A series of shattered mountains are carved into this chime. When activated, you touch the chime against an unattended object, casting [_shatter_](/Spells.aspx?ID=279) upon the object, ignoring the object's Hardness if it's 6 or lower. You can use this chime once per day.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs=""
>
[Final Blade](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1094)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Night of the Gray Death</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Death" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=40" />
<trait label="Evil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=64" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Night of the Gray Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=99) pg. 53, [World Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=6) pg. 126

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 40
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A Large or smaller restrained or willing creature can be executed by a _final blade_. 

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (Interact);     **Effect** You execute a creature restrained beneath the blade. The creature you execute takes 11d10 slashing damage and must succeed at a DC 45 Fortitude save or be decapitated as though it suffered a critical hit with a natural 20 from a _+3 major striking [vorpal](/Equipment.aspx?ID=308)_ scythe. The soul of a creature executed with a _final blade_ is trapped, and the creature can't be returned to life, not even by [_miracle_](/Spells.aspx?ID=196) or similar magic. A _final blade_ can hold any number of souls in this way, and a sufficient number of captured souls forms a monster called a [gray death](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1641).

**Destruction** A final blade can be destroyed via the rare ritual, [blunt the final blade](/Rituals.aspx?ID=69).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Final Blade

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Nosoi Charm](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1095)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Night of the Gray Death</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Night of the Gray Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=99) pg. 53

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Nosois serve as scribes and messengers to psychopomps. They are rare outside of the Boneyard, so it's common for [psychopomps](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=83) venturing to other planes to carry a nosoi facsimile to aid in their travels. This tiny icon of a masked raven emits twittering sounds in perfect accompaniment to your performances. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks to play an instrument, orate, or sing. You can cast [_sending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=272) once per day as a divine innate spell. 

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> Interact;     **Effect** You twist the base of the charm, and the twittering it emits becomes helpful words to boost your arguments. You lose the item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks and gain a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks to make [Requests](/Actions.aspx?ID=51) until you use this activation again.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You gain [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) as an imprecise sense with a range of 30 feet for 1 hour as long as you continue to hold the charm. This allows you to sense the life force that animates living creatures and the perverse force that animates the dead, though you can't distinguish between the two.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Limited"
>
Nosoi Charm
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Night of the Gray Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=99) pg. 53

**Price** 1,900 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Limited"
>
Nosoi Charm (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Night of the Gray Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=99) pg. 53

**Price** 19,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) or [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) is +3. You gain [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) as long as you are holding the charm, and you don't need to activate it.
name: Nosoi Charm

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Nosoi Charm](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1095)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Night of the Gray Death</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Night of the Gray Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=99) pg. 53

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Nosois serve as scribes and messengers to psychopomps. They are rare outside of the Boneyard, so it's common for [psychopomps](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=83) venturing to other planes to carry a nosoi facsimile to aid in their travels. This tiny icon of a masked raven emits twittering sounds in perfect accompaniment to your performances. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks to play an instrument, orate, or sing. You can cast [_sending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=272) once per day as a divine innate spell. 

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> Interact;     **Effect** You twist the base of the charm, and the twittering it emits becomes helpful words to boost your arguments. You lose the item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks and gain a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks to make [Requests](/Actions.aspx?ID=51) until you use this activation again.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You gain [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) as an imprecise sense with a range of 30 feet for 1 hour as long as you continue to hold the charm. This allows you to sense the life force that animates living creatures and the perverse force that animates the dead, though you can't distinguish between the two.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blade Launcher](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1096)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 64

**Price** 18 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A blade launcher has a bow-like assembly mounted on a handled frame. The frame can be configured to fire either a [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3), [dart](/Weapons.aspx?ID=68), [shuriken](/Weapons.aspx?ID=79), or [starknife](/Weapons.aspx?ID=44). Configuring a blade launcher requires three Interact actions. Once properly configured, loading an appropriate [thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=195) weapon into a blade launcher requires a single Interact action. A blade launcher can't fire weapons for which it's not currently configured. A weapon fired with a blade launcher loses the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [monk](/Traits.aspx?ID=112), [thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=195), and [versatile](/Traits.aspx?ID=200) traits, if it has them, and has a range increment of 40 feet. Due to losing the thrown weapon trait, [_returning_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301) and most other effects that allow a weapon to return don't function; even if a launched weapon did return, you'd still need to load it into the blade launcher with an Interact action to fire the blade launcher again.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Blade Launcher

    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bomb Launcher](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1097)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 64

**Price** 20 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This long, hollow tube is held in two hands and braced over the shoulder. Inside, it contains a small metal basket sized to hold [alchemical bombs](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&Subcategory=7). A chute in the top delivers an alchemical bomb into the internal basket, while a lever on the underside pulls the basket back and locks it in place. Loading an alchemical bomb into a bomb launcher requires a single Interact action. With a pull of a trigger, the basket speeds forward, allowing you to Strike with the loaded alchemical bomb over long distances. Bombs fired with a bomb launcher have a [range increment](/Traits.aspx?ID=248) of 60 feet.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bomb Launcher

    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Deployable Cover](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1098)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 64

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This thick mat of canvas, foliage, and wood is mounted on a tripod of flexible metal struts, folded into a baton- like shape, and clamped shut. You can rapidly deploy it on the ground with an Interact action to create cover. Deployable cover completely blocks one edge of the chosen square, allowing you (and others) to gain standard cover when you use the [Take Cover](/Actions.aspx?ID=2307) action. Before it can be used again, deployable cover must be carefully folded and clamped shut, which takes 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</traits>

</traits>

</row>

---

Specially crafted to protect against bullet fire, a ballistic cover also works against other physical projectiles, such as arrows, bolts, and [thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=195) weapons. While a creature has cover from Taking Cover behind a ballistic cover, it gains resistance 2 to piercing damage from ranged weapons and ranged [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attacks.
name: Deployable Cover

---

This wooden, pistol-like device features a large reel coiled with 100 feet of thin metal cord and can fire a [grappling hook](/Equipment.aspx?ID=23) up to 100 feet. To reload the grappling gun, you must manually recoil the cord by turning the reel's crank, and then lock in the grappling hook. Reloading a grappling gun takes 1 minute.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Net Launcher](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1100)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 65

**Price** 16 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wide, hollow tube is two to three feet long and fires an unattached [net](/Equipment.aspx?ID=681) at much greater range than one can be thrown. A net launcher can be wielded while propped up on your shoulder or cradled under your arm. A net must be carefully folded to be launched without tangling. Properly loading a net into a net launcher takes 1 minute. A net fired with a net launcher can target a Medium or smaller creature within 40 feet, rather than 20 feet.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Net Launcher

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wind-up Wings](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1101)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Clockwork" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=331" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 65

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to a  thrown  weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These delicate clockwork wings enable thrown weapons to soar through the air at high speeds, performing turns and other aerial maneuvers midflight. Wind-up wings also have different types of special effects depending on the type. Wind-up wings must be attached to a [thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=195) weapon and wound to function. A thrown weapon with an attached set of wind-up wings can't have anything else attached to it, or the wings cease to function.

 Attaching or detaching a pair of wind-up wings to a thrown weapon requires a [repair kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=43), and the process takes 10 minutes. Winding an attached pair of wind-up wings takes three Interact actions. There are a variety of wind-up wings, with different effects.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wind-up Wings (Flutterback)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 65

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you make a thrown Strike with the weapon to which a pair of flutterback wind-up wings are attached, and the wings are wound, the wings fly the weapon back to your hand after the Strike is complete. If your hands are full when the weapon returns, the wings hover in place three feet above the ground. At the end of your turn, the wings are wound down; they don't function again until wound. If you aren't holding the weapon when the flutterback wind-up wings become wound down, the weapon falls to the ground.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wind-up Wings (Homing)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 65

**Price** 1,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you make a thrown Strike with the weapon to which a pair of homing wind-up wings are attached, and the wings are wound, the wings seek out your target, flying through cover and avoiding obstacles. You ignore the target's [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition and reduce the target's cover by one step (lesser cover to no cover, standard cover to lesser cover, or greater cover to standard cover). After the Strike is complete, the wings are wound down; they don't function again until wound.
name: Wind-up Wings

    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ablative Armor Plating](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1102)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 66

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to armor or traveler's clothing

**Bulk** 1
</row>

**Access**  Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Ablative armor plating is additional plating, combined with springs, clever clockwork, and improvised padding, to absorb damage by knocking the additional pieces free. The pieces slowly come apart on their own with time and movement. The process of attaching the plating takes 10 minutes and grants the wearer of the armor the listed temporary Hit Points for the stated duration or until lost. Removing the plating early destroys it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ablative Armor Plating](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1102)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 66

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to armor or traveler's clothing

**Bulk** 1
</row>

**Access**  Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Ablative armor plating is additional plating, combined with springs, clever clockwork, and improvised padding, to absorb damage by knocking the additional pieces free. The pieces slowly come apart on their own with time and movement. The process of attaching the plating takes 10 minutes and grants the wearer of the armor the listed temporary Hit Points for the stated duration or until lost. Removing the plating early destroys it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ablative Shield Plating](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1103)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 67

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to a shield

**Bulk** 1
</row>

**Access**  Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Ablative shield plating is an amalgam device that provides a layer of additional protection for a shield. Ablative shield plating is cobbled together from parts of other shields, wires, scraps, and bits of detritus, adding mass to a shield. The process of attaching ablative shield plating takes 10 minutes, and you can't attach ablative shield plating to a shield that has any other attached item, such as a [shield boss](/Weapons.aspx?ID=41) or [shield spikes](/Weapons.aspx?ID=42). When the shield is damaged, this additional material crumples, breaks, and falls apart, absorbing some of the energy of the blow. Otherwise, the additional material slowly crumbles over time. Applying the plating grants the shield temporary Hit Points that last for the stated duration or until lost. Removing the plating early destroys it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 67

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to a shield

**Bulk** 1
</row>

**Access**  Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Ablative shield plating is an amalgam device that provides a layer of additional protection for a shield. Ablative shield plating is cobbled together from parts of other shields, wires, scraps, and bits of detritus, adding mass to a shield. The process of attaching ablative shield plating takes 10 minutes, and you can't attach ablative shield plating to a shield that has any other attached item, such as a [shield boss](/Weapons.aspx?ID=41) or [shield spikes](/Weapons.aspx?ID=42). When the shield is damaged, this additional material crumples, breaks, and falls apart, absorbing some of the energy of the blow. Otherwise, the additional material slowly crumbles over time. Applying the plating grants the shield temporary Hit Points that last for the stated duration or until lost. Removing the plating early destroys it.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blast Boots](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1104)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 67

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn (attached to footwear or feet)

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access**  Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These sets of rockets come in pairs and strap onto existing footwear (or a creature's feet). Inserting them and aligning them properly takes 1 minute. When you Activate the blast boots, you can [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=36) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=37), without the need to Stride first. Higher-level versions increase the distance of your High Jump or Long Jump.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blast Boots (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 67

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blast Boots (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 67

**Price** 11 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

When you Activate the moderate blast boots to High Jump, you can increase the vertical distance of your High Jump by up to 30 feet. When you Activate them to Long Jump, you can increase the horizontal distance of your Long Jump by up to 45 feet.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blast Boots (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 67

**Price** 140 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

When you Activate the greater blast boots to High Jump, you can increase the vertical distance of your High Jump by up to 50 feet. When you Activate them to Long Jump, you can increase the horizontal distance of your Long Jump by up to 75 feet. Additionally, for 1 minute, the greater blast boots continue to boost your jumps. Every time you [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=81) during the duration, you can move 30 feet in any direction, or your normal Leap distance, whichever is further.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blast Boots (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 67

**Price** 2,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

When you Activate the major blast boots to High Jump, you can increase the vertical distance of your High Jump by up to 80 feet. When you Activate them to Long Jump, you can increase the horizontal distance of your Long Jump by up to 120 feet. Additionally, for 1 minute, the major blast boots remain active, allowing you to clumsily fly through the air for the duration. You gain a 30-foot Fly speed for the duration, but any time you Fly, you are [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) until the start of your next turn.
name: Blast Boots

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blast Boots](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1104)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 67

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn (attached to footwear or feet)

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access**  Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These sets of rockets come in pairs and strap onto existing footwear (or a creature's feet). Inserting them and aligning them properly takes 1 minute. When you Activate the blast boots, you can [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=36) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=37), without the need to Stride first. Higher-level versions increase the distance of your High Jump or Long Jump.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blast Boots (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 67

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blast Boots (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 67

**Price** 11 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

When you Activate the moderate blast boots to High Jump, you can increase the vertical distance of your High Jump by up to 30 feet. When you Activate them to Long Jump, you can increase the horizontal distance of your Long Jump by up to 45 feet.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blast Boots (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 67

**Price** 140 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

When you Activate the greater blast boots to High Jump, you can increase the vertical distance of your High Jump by up to 50 feet. When you Activate them to Long Jump, you can increase the horizontal distance of your Long Jump by up to 75 feet. Additionally, for 1 minute, the greater blast boots continue to boost your jumps. Every time you [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=81) during the duration, you can move 30 feet in any direction, or your normal Leap distance, whichever is further.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blast Boots (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 67

**Price** 2,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

When you Activate the major blast boots to High Jump, you can increase the vertical distance of your High Jump by up to 80 feet. When you Activate them to Long Jump, you can increase the horizontal distance of your Long Jump by up to 120 feet. Additionally, for 1 minute, the major blast boots remain active, allowing you to clumsily fly through the air for the duration. You gain a 30-foot Fly speed for the duration, but any time you Fly, you are [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) until the start of your next turn.
name: Blast Boots (Moderate)

    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cauterizing Torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1105)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 67

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access**  Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This small, clockwork torch device uses a trigger-operated sparker to ignite a flow of flammable gas, creating a short, hot flame capable of rapidly cauterizing wounds and helping to stop bleeding. The cauterizing torch is applied to yourself or an adjacent target. The target attempts an immediate flat check to end any [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) effect with the lower DC for particularly effective assistance.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Cauterizing Torch

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Chameleon Suit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1106)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Clockwork" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=331" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 67

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn over armor and clothes

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** Characters from Absalom, New Thassilon, Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, or especially technological regions of Arcadia like the Deadshot Lands have access to uncommon clockwork items from the first two chapters of this book. Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This complex clockwork outerwear, worn over armor or other clothing, is composed of thin, interlocking scales of myriad colors attached to numerous clockwork gears that can rotate between all visible colors and shades. 

When activated, the clockwork gears work double-time to temporarily switch and adjust the scales to match the exact coloration of the area around you, allowing you to blend in as long as you stay still. For the duration, you can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) without needing cover or concealment to do so. This doesn't allow you to [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63) without ending your movement in cover or concealment, however, as the clockwork flips over and adjusts the scales to match your background as you move, giving the impression of rippling waves of color and revealing your movement. Once the duration expires, the overworked clockwork and scales fall apart.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Chameleon Suit
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 67

**Price** 16 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The effect lasts for 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Chameleon Suit (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 67

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The effect lasts for 10 minutes.

level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Chameleon Suit (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The effect lasts for 1 hour.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Chameleon Suit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1106)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Clockwork" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=331" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 67

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn over armor and clothes

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** Characters from Absalom, New Thassilon, Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, or especially technological regions of Arcadia like the Deadshot Lands have access to uncommon clockwork items from the first two chapters of this book. Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This complex clockwork outerwear, worn over armor or other clothing, is composed of thin, interlocking scales of myriad colors attached to numerous clockwork gears that can rotate between all visible colors and shades. 

When activated, the clockwork gears work double-time to temporarily switch and adjust the scales to match the exact coloration of the area around you, allowing you to blend in as long as you stay still. For the duration, you can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) without needing cover or concealment to do so. This doesn't allow you to [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63) without ending your movement in cover or concealment, however, as the clockwork flips over and adjusts the scales to match your background as you move, giving the impression of rippling waves of color and revealing your movement. Once the duration expires, the overworked clockwork and scales fall apart.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The effect lasts for 1 minute.

**Bulk** L

</row>

---

The effect lasts for 10 minutes.

<traits>

</traits>

<title

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Clockwork Goggles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1107)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Clockwork" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=331" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 68

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** Characters from Absalom, New Thassilon, Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, or especially technological regions of Arcadia like the Deadshot Lands have access to uncommon clockwork items from the first two chapters of this book. Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This heavy set of mechanical goggles contains a variety of different lenses for different situations, including lenses that filter light at different intensities and frequencies and even some that capture heat and other stimuli. Clockwork mechanisms can swap between the lenses rapidly as needed. However, many of these lenses can only capture a single stimulus once and then are forever etched with it, making them unsuitable for long-term use. When activated, the clockwork mechanism on the goggles rapidly switches between the different lenses, granting you the benefits of all of them for a brief period of time.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Clockwork Goggles
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 68

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, you gain [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416) for 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Clockwork Goggles (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 68

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, you gain low-light vision and [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415) for 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Clockwork Goggles (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 68

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, you gain low-light vision, darkvision, and the effects of [_see invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) for 10 minutes.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you Activate a cryomister, you can either place it in an adjacent square or toss it up to 20 feet away. Once you've done so, the metallic device instantly disperses a heavy mist, settling quickly on nearby flat surfaces and forming a sheet of ice. The cryomister creates a burst with a radius depending on the type, making the affected area difficult terrain for 1 minute; at the GM's discretion, however, this duration might be reduced in unusually hot conditions or increased in unusually cold ones. If an affected square takes fire damage, the difficult terrain is removed. Though not designed as a weapon, the cryomister's rapidly cooling mist deals cold [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage depending on the type to creatures in the area on activation. Creatures attempting to move through the affected area can attempt an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) check with a DC depending on the type to ignore the difficult terrain and move at their normal Speed. Throwing the cryomister onto the surface of a liquid creates a floating piece of ice for the same duration, capable of supporting one or more creatures of the listed size.

markdown:
**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 68 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_ **Price** 30 gp **Usage** worn **Bulk** 1 **Activate**Interact --- This rare gadget uses Stasian technology to grant someone a burst of activity, though its use can be painful. The electromuscular stimulator must be carefully attached to you, requiring 1 minute to do so. You can attach an electromuscular stimulator to yourself. When you Activate an attached electromuscular stimulator, roll a [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) check, using the Crafting modifier of the creature who attached the stimulator to you, with a DC equal to the standard DC for your level. The effects of the activation depend on the result of the Crafting check. **Critical Success** You gain the [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) condition for 1 minute and can use the extra action each round only to Stride or Strike.
**Success** As critical success, but you also take 3 [persistent electrical damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
**Failure** You gain the quickened condition for 2 rounds and can use the extra action each round only to Stride or Strike. You take 3 persistent electrical damage.
**Critical Failure** You take 2d6 electrical damage and 3 persistent electrical damage.
name: Electromuscular Stimulator

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Explosive Mine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1111)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 69

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access**  Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you Activate an explosive mine, you place it on the ground in a square within your reach, priming it to explode when a creature steps on that square. If you have time in advance to prepare, you can place the mine on the ground before activating it, and you or an ally can try to use [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) to [Conceal an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=61) before you Activate the mine. If you don't Conceal the mine, its position is obvious at a glance. 

Once activated, the mine is primed to explode when enough pressure is placed on the square where it's located. </title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 69

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access**  Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Flare beacons create an incredibly bright light for a brief period of time. They are often used to signal others to the beacon's location, to coordinate assaults, to request rescue, or for other similar reasons. Higher-level beacons have a radius so large that they can be seen from miles away at night. When you Activate a flare beacon, you can either place it on the ground in a space within your reach or toss it up to 60 feet straight up. The beacon then sparks into being, casting bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light in the next 20 feet for 1 minute. A flare beacon in the air falls at a rate of 10 feet per round. Creatures adjacent to a flare beacon must succeed at a DC 15 Fortitude save or be [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) until they are no longer adjacent to it.

The beacon shines bright light in a 100-foot radius and dim light in the next 100 feet. Additionally, on a critical failure, they are also [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 round.

Flare beacons create an incredibly bright light for a brief period of time. They are often used to signal others to the beacon's location, to coordinate assaults, to request rescue, or for other similar reasons. Higher-level beacons have a radius so large that they can be seen from miles away at night. When you Activate a flare beacon, you can either place it on the ground in a space within your reach or toss it up to 60 feet straight up. The beacon then sparks into being, casting bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light in the next 20 feet for 1 minute. A flare beacon in the air falls at a rate of 10 feet per round. Creatures adjacent to a flare beacon must succeed at a DC 15 Fortitude save or be [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) until they are no longer adjacent to it.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Gadget Skates](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1113)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Clockwork" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=331" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 70

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

**Price** 21 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn (attached to footwear or feet)

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** Characters from Absalom, New Thassilon, Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, or especially technological regions of Arcadia like the Deadshot Lands have access to uncommon clockwork items from the first two chapters of this book. Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row><actions string="Single Action" /> Interact ([move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Gadget skates are metal devices that come in pairs and strap onto existing footwear (or a creature's feet).

 When you Activate gadget skates, clockwork gears reveal small wheels that propel you along the ground, moving you up to twice your Speed. During this movement, you must succeed at a DC 20 [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) check to turn up to 90 degrees, and you can't turn more than once or more than 90 degrees. During the activation and after the activation is complete, you can't Stride, Step, or otherwise move on the ground until the end of your next turn. However, you can use this movement instead of a Stride to perform a [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=36) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=37) just after the activation ends, in the direction of your last movement using the gadget skates.

 You can Sustain the Activation of your gadget skates for up to 1 minute. Starting on the turn after you Activate the gadget skates, the first time you Sustain the Activation each turn, you gain the effects of the activation again: you move up to twice your Speed in the same direction as your last movement with the gadget skates, and the restrictions continue to apply until you choose to cease Sustaining the Activation or you reach the maximum duration. Once the effect ends, the skates fall away and you can move normally.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Gadget Skates

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** Characters from Absalom, New Thassilon, Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, or especially technological regions of Arcadia like the Deadshot Lands have access to uncommon clockwork items from the first two chapters of this book. Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Gecko pads are thin, clockwork devices shaped like palms, which can be strapped onto existing handwear or a creature's hands. When activated, they whir to life and improve your grip on surfaces, while slowly releasing a sticky substance stored within the device's surface to help you climb. The pads give you a climb Speed equal to your Speed as long as your hands are free.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Gecko Pads
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Access** Characters from Absalom, New Thassilon, Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, or especially technological regions of Arcadia like the Deadshot Lands have access to uncommon clockwork items from the first two chapters of this book. Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

</row>

</column>

---

Gecko pads are thin, clockwork devices shaped like palms, which can be strapped onto existing handwear or a creature's hands. When activated, they whir to life and improve your grip on surfaces, while slowly releasing a sticky substance stored within the device's surface to help you climb. The pads give you a climb Speed equal to your Speed as long as your hands are free.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Impact Foam Chassis](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1115)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 70

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn over armor and clothes

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access**  Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row>see below</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

While you're wearing an impact foam chassis wrapped around your body, it Activates automatically whenever you fall at least 10 feet, causing a layer of soft, flexible impact foam to expand beneath and around you to cushion the fall. The impact foam decreases the falling damage by up to the listed amount, taking damage equal to the amount of falling damage the foam reduced. If this is enough to destroy the foam, it disperses immediately. However, if the foam has any Hit Points remaining, it remains surrounding you for up to 1 minute. While you're surrounded in foam, you are [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) and have standard cover against other creatures, and other creatures have standard cover against you. The foam has an [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) DC of 15; a creature can also remove it by spending three Interact actions. You or others can also attack the foam (the foam has an AC of 12), which disperses if reduced to 0 Hit Points.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Impact Foam Chassis (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 70

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

A lesser impact foam chassis prevents up to 20 falling damage and has 20 Hit Points.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Impact Foam Chassis (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 70

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

A moderate impact foam chassis prevents up to 50 falling damage and has 40 Hit Points.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Impact Foam Chassis (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 70

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

A greater impact foam chassis prevents up to 100 falling damage and has 100 Hit Points.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Impact Foam Chassis (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 70

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

A major impact foam chassis prevents up to 200 falling damage and has 200 Hit Points.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Impact Foam Chassis](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1115)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 70

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn over armor and clothes

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access**  Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row>see below</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

While you're wearing an impact foam chassis wrapped around your body, it Activates automatically whenever you fall at least 10 feet, causing a layer of soft, flexible impact foam to expand beneath and around you to cushion the fall. The impact foam decreases the falling damage by up to the listed amount, taking damage equal to the amount of falling damage the foam reduced. If this is enough to destroy the foam, it disperses immediately. However, if the foam has any Hit Points remaining, it remains surrounding you for up to 1 minute. While you're surrounded in foam, you are [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) and have standard cover against other creatures, and other creatures have standard cover against you. The foam has an [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) DC of 15; a creature can also remove it by spending three Interact actions. You or others can also attack the foam (the foam has an AC of 12), which disperses if reduced to 0 Hit Points.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Instant Spy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1116)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Clockwork" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=331" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 70

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** Characters from Absalom, New Thassilon, Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, or especially technological regions of Arcadia like the Deadshot Lands have access to uncommon clockwork items from the first two chapters of this book. Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An instant spy is a tiny clockwork device that is small enough to be easily hidden. It contains the same audio-recording mechanisms as a [clockwork spy](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1095), as well as a short-lived gemstone that can store up to 1 hour of sound to play back later.

 Once you have activated the instant spy, it surreptitiously begins recording all the sounds around it for an hour. In general, it can hear sounds that are clearly audible in the location where you activated it, but not sounds that would require a Perception check with a DC of 10 or greater to hear. The GM determines exactly what the instant spy can hear and record, as well as whether or not the recording is clear in a situation where the original sound is quiet, distorted, or intentionally obscured.

 When the recording is complete, once within the next 24 hours, you or another creature can use an Interact action to play back the stored audio in its entirety, after which the gem crumbles to dust. If no one plays the recorded sounds back within 1 day, the gem crumbles into dust anyway. This prevents the information from falling into the wrong hands later. It's typical to use the [Conceal an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=61) action to hide an instant spy in a location once you've activated it, since if you don't hide it, it's relatively easy to discover in a thorough search, despite the fact that it's small and relatively unobtrusive.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Instant Spy

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Magnetic Suit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1117)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 71

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 10 minutes

**Usage** worn over armor and clothes

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This magnetized suit is strapped to your body over your armor or clothes. When you Activate it, you must choose whether to set it to attract or repel.

 While set to attract, you take a –1 status penalty to your AC against attacks made by metal weapons, while allies within a 10-foot emanation other than you gain a +1 status bonus to their AC. If set to repel, you gain a +1 status bonus to AC against attacks made with metal weapons. While attracting metal, you gain a +1 status bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33) metal objects and [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) metal creatures and a –1 status penalty to Athletics checks to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) metal creatures. While repelling metal, you gain a +1 status bonus to Athletics checks to Shove metal creatures, but you take a –1 status penalty to Climb metal objects and Grapple metal creatures.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

---

The area that gains an AC bonus when attracting is a 15-foot emanation, and the status bonuses and penalties increase from 1 to 2.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 71

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access**  Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you Activate a smoke fan, it creates a cloud of colored smoke. The smoke fills a 5-foot radius. The creator chooses the smoke's color when creating the smoke fan. Creatures within the smoke's area are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4), and all other creatures are concealed to them. The smoke lasts for 1 minute or until dissipated by a strong wind.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
